what_it_does: This file is an area landscape/inventory document for the `surface/` directory: it describes the operator-facing React 18 + Vite TypeScript instrument, its sub-areas, per-file roles, event wiring, API endpoint map, SurfaceState schema, index.html script load order, CSS z-index stack, known gaps, and design surprises.
observations: Documents a React 18 + Vite app at port 5174 that proxies `/api` to a bridge at port 8770.
Provides a per-file inventory covering App.tsx, layouts, faces, wheel views, toggles, stores, library files, tokens, CSS, public gallery scripts, public DNA JSON, and build scripts.
Maps window-level CustomEvents such as `projection:select`, `projection:aim`, `gallery:verb`, `decision:open`, and `ui:point` with their dispatchers and listeners.
Lists `/api` endpoints consumed by the surface, including stream, projection, context, territory, decisions, sessions, channels, tools, layers, layer-dims, resolve, and claude/turn.
Defines `SurfaceState` with roughly 30 fields covering projection, binding, embedding, space, resolution, selection, view mode, nucleation, centre, poles, live state, time scrub, and notice.
Specifies a strict, ordered `<script>` load sequence in `index.html` for DNA gallery modules before the deferred React module.
Catalogs 8 documented gaps/incomplete seams and 12 surprises/design decisions.
Uses a module-level subscribe store pattern across decisions, sessions, channels, tools, and Locus rather than React Context or Zustand.
Emphasizes operator-law: the surface must translate all technical names/addresses into human meaning and never show machine IDs or raw paths.
Includes a CSS z-index stack and cross-references to `counterpart/design/` and `build-prep/front-interface/` as sync sources.
standouts: The surface is framed as 'Project: Instrument' — the singular operator-facing instrument, distinct from the canvas and from developer tools.
`GalleryMount.tsx` is deliberately kept always-in-DOM (visibility toggled by CSS) so DNA's container refs remain stable.
`RightHand.tsx` is a persistent draggable 'V' handle with a 6-verb fan, brain panel, note composer, greeting, and guided tour.
`Tether.tsx` draws a bowed SVG arc via a `requestAnimationFrame` loop that writes directly to path attributes, explicitly avoiding React re-renders per frame.
The `sync-gallery.mjs` predev/prebuild script fails loud if any DNA source file is missing, blocking stale builds.
conventions: Follows a structured area-ledger format: purpose, major sub-areas, per-file inventory, event wiring, API map, state map, load order, gaps/surprises, z-index, cross-refs.
Uses registry-true and operator-law vocabulary consistently throughout.
Uses a lightweight module-level subscribe store pattern with `ensureStarted()` guards.
Breaks typical React unmount behavior by keeping the gallery mount always rendered.
Breaks typical brain-to-surface address exposure by sending opaque pointable tokens to the brain and keeping token-to-address mapping client-side.
concerns: `channelStore.ensureStarted()` is documented as empty, so channels never auto-load on first subscriber.
`ToolsPanel` DNA tool-card result rendering is described as unimplemented (GAP 4).
`drive` and `generate` verbs are dispatched from `RightHand.tsx` but `App.tsx` only shows a Notice rather than performing real work.
`Taste.tsx` typeface and pigment choices are applied to the DOM but not persisted to `localStorage`.
Channel `members` normalization happens both in the host (`ChannelView`) and in the DNA adapter (`face-adapters.js`).
`resolveAllocation.ts` and the `session-recall` `LENS_ROUTE` are described as committed-not-live or waiting on a checkpoint bounce.
The legacy `gallery:direction` event alias is still emitted alongside `gallery:verb`, with a TODO to remove it.
notes: This is the master landscape doc for the operator surface instrument — a dense, human-readable map of what lives in `surface/`, how it talks to the bridge, and where the known seams are.
questions: Which exact files in `counterpart/design/` correspond to each public gallery module beyond the listed names?
Is the bridge at port 8770 the same backend as the canvas app or a separate service?
What is the 'checkpoint bounce' that activates the `session-recall` `LENS_ROUTE`?
How do the `drive` and `generate` verbs differ in intended behavior once implemented?
What are the full response schemas for `/api/projection` and `/api/territory`?
Are there sibling `AREA-*.md` files in `company-landscape/` and how do they relate to this one?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to document the `surface/` area; the file does exactly that, with extensive per-file detail, wiring maps, and explicit gap notes.
apparent_themes: Operator-law: surfaces must translate machine identifiers into human meaning.
Registry-true: UI choices derive from API/registry data rather than hardcoded sets.
Fail-loud: sync errors, notices, and documented gaps are surfaced rather than swallowed.
Stable DNA integration: always-in-DOM host, deterministic placement, strict script load order.
Explicit seams: incomplete work and design oddities are recorded rather than hidden.
One-engine collapse: a single archetype renderer and single brain core serve multiple faces.
intention_signals: Provide a single authoritative, human-readable map of the surface instrument so a maintainer can navigate its many files, understand event and API contracts, and know where incomplete work remains — treating the codebase itself as a living fabric to be perceived rather than merely executed.
novelty: The document is an interpretive layer over code, blending architectural narrative with precise inventories (z-index, event wiring, API endpoints, state fields) and an explicit 'gaps and surprises' section that encodes design rationale and known incompleteness rather than pretending the system is finished.
contribution: It contributes a high-level, human-navigable index of the surface area, its contracts (events, APIs, state), its design principles (operator-law, registry-true, fail-loud), and its documented seams — making it possible to orient across roughly 50 files without reading each one first.
summary_for_embedding: AREA-surface.md is a landscape document that inventories the React+Vite operator surface instrument under `/home/tim/company/surface/`, describing its files, components, stores, event wiring, API endpoints, SurfaceState schema, DNA gallery load order, CSS z-index stack, known gaps, and design surprises.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give maintainers a single readable map of the surface instrument's structure, contracts, and remaining seams.
